methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $3.3B - $7.6B.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $7.6B

data.narrative:
Nuro, a Series G autonomous delivery drone company founded by Dave Ferguson and Jiajun Zhu, is evaluated against a peer set of four private and public-equivalent companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe includes Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), Joby Aviation (public, $6.9B), Archer Aviation (public, $3.3B), and Elroy Air, providing cross-stage visibility within the frontier logistics space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x multiplier to Nuro's last post-money valuation of $6.0B, implying $7.5B. Peer-median triangulation across the three priced comparables yields $6.9B, with a range spanning $3.3B to $7.6B. The stage-progression method reflects expected value creation in advanced funding rounds; peer analysis anchors valuation to observable market pricing for similarly positioned autonomous vehicle platforms at comparable or later stages.

The two methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $5.8B to $8.6B, with a median of $7.2B. This range sits moderately above Nuro's current $6.0B post-money valuation, consistent with typical Series G progression. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity for autonomous delivery operations, capital intensity through path to profitability, and relative commercialization progress versus peers. The wide peer range ($3.3B–$7.6B) reflects material variance in market assessments of autonomous vehicle company viability.
